ubication: The state of being in a place; local relation.
ubiety: The state of being in a place; local relation.
ubiquitary: Existing every where, or in all places.
ubiquity: Existence in all places or every where at the same time; omnipresence. The ubiquity of God is not disputed by those who admit his existence.
udder: The breast of a female; but the word is applied chiefly or wholly to the glandular organ of female breasts, in which the milk is secreted and retained for the nourishment of their young, commonly called the bag, in cows and other quadrupeds.
uglily: In an ugly manner; with deformity.
ugliness: Total want of beauty; deformity of person; as old age and ugliness.
Turpitude of mind; moral depravity; lothesomeness. Their dull ribaldry must be offensive to any one who does not, for the sake of the sin, pardon the ugliness of its circumstances.
ugly: Deformed; offensive to the sight; contrary to beauty; hateful; as an ugly person; an ugly face. O I have pass’d a miserable night, so full of ugly sights, of ghastly dreams. Fellow, begone; I cannot bear thy sight; this news hath made thee a most ugly man.
ukase: In Russia, a proclamation or imperial order published.
ulcer: A sore; a solution of continuity in any of the soft parts of the body, attend with a secretion of pus or some kind of discharge. Ulcers on the lungs are seldom healed.
ulcerate: To be formed into an ulcer; to become ulcerous.
To affect with an ulcer or with ulcers.
ulcerating: Turning to an ulcer; generating ulcers.
ulceration: The process of forming into an ulcer, or the process of becoming ulcerous.
An ulcer; a morbid sore that discharges pus or other fluid.
ulcerous: Having the nature or character of an ulcer; discharging purulent or other matter.
Affected with an ulcer or with ulcers.
ule-tree: In botany the Castilla, a genus of trees, whose milky juice yields that king of elastic gum, called by the Mexicans ule.
ullage: In commerce, the wantage of casks of liquor, or what a cask wants of being full.
ulmin: A substance obtained from the elm tree, of very singular properties. It resembles gum, but is hard of a black color, and considerably bitter. In its original state it is soluble in water, and insoluble in alcohol or ether; but when nitric or oxymuriatic acid is poured into its solution, it changes into a resinous substance no longer soluble in water, but soluble in alcohol. A substance originally obtained in the state of an exsudation from the elm; but it is found to be a constituent of the bark of almost all trees.
ulnar: Pertaining to the ulna or cubit; as the ulnar nerve.
ulterior: Further; as ulterior demands; ulterior propositions. What ulterior measures will be adopted is uncertain.
In geography, being or situated beyond or on the further side of any line or boundary; opposed to citerior, or hither.
ultimate: Furthest; most remote; extreme. We have not yet arrived at the ultimate point of progression.

Final; being that to which all the rest is directed, as to the main object. The ultimate end of our actions should be the glory of God, or the display of his exalted excellence. The ultimate end and aim of men is to be happy, and to attain to this end, we must yield that obedience which will honor the law and character of God.
Last in a train of consequences; intended in the last resort. Many actions apt to procure fame, are not conductive to this our ultimate happiness.
Last; terminating; being at the furthest point.
The last into which a substance can be resolved; constituent.
ultimately: Finally; at last; in the end of last consequence. Afflictions often tend to correct immoral habits, and ultimately prove blessings.
ultimatum: In diplomacy, the final propositions, conditions or terms offered as the basis of a treaty; the most favorable terms that a negotiator can offer, and the rejection of which usually puts an end to negotiation. It is sometimes used in the plural.
ultramarine: Situated or being beyond the sea.
a beautiful and durable sky-blue; a color formed of the mineral called lapis lazuli, and consisting of little else than oxyd of iron.

Azure-stone. Ultramarine ashes, a pigment which is the residuum of lapis lazuli, after the ultramarine has been extracted. Their appearance is that of the ultramarine, a little tinged with red, and diluted with white.
ultramontane: Being beyond the mountain. Thus France, with regard to Italy, is an ultramontane country. Bouffin is the only ultramontane painter whom the Italians seem to envy.
ultramundane: Being beyond the world, or beyond the limits of our system.
ululate: to how, as a dog or wolf.
ululation: a howling, as of the wolf or dog.
umbel: In botany, a particular mode of inflorescence or flowering, which consists of a number of flower-stalks or rays, nearly equal in length, spreading from a common center, their summits forming a level, convex, or even globose surface, more rarely a concave one, as in the carrot. it is simple or compound; in the latter, each peduncle bears another little umbel, umbellet or umbellicle. Umbel is sometimes called a rundle, from its roundness.
umbellar: Pertaining to an umbel; having the form of an umbel.
umbellate: Bearing umbels; consisting of an umbel; growing on an umbel; as umbellate plants or flowers.
umbellated: Bearing umbels; consisting of an umbel; growing on an umbel; as umbellate plants or flowers.
umbelliferous: Producing the inflorescence called an umbel; bearing umbels; as umbelliferous plants.
umber: In natural history, an ore of iron, a fossil of a brown, yellowish, or blackish brown color, so called from Ombria in Italy, where it was first obtained. It is used in painting. A specimen from Cyprus afforded, of a hundred parts, 48 parts of oxyd of iron, 20 of oxyd of manganese, the remainder silex, alumin and water.
A fowl of Africa, called the African crow. The Scopus umbretta, a fowl of the grallic order, inhabiting Africa.
A fish of the truttaceous kind, called the grayling, or thymallus; a fresh water fish of a fine taste.
To color with umber; to shade or darken.
umbilic: Pertaining to the navel; as umbilical vessels; umbilical region. Umbilical points, in mathematics, the same as foci. Umbilical vessels, in vegetables, are the small vessels which pass from the heart of the seed into the side seed-lobes, and are supposed to imbibe the saccharine, farinaceous or oily matter which is to support the new vegetable in its germination and infant growth.
umbilicate: Navel-shaped; formed in the middle like a navel; as a flower, fruit, or leaf.
umbilicated: Navel-shaped; formed in the middle like a navel; as a flower, fruit, or leaf.
umbo: The boss or protuberant part of a shield.
umboldilite: A newly discovered Vesuvian mineral, whose primitive form is a right rectangular prism, with a square base, its color brown, inclining to yellowish or greenish yellow.
umbra: A fish caught in the Mediterranean, generally about 12 or 14 inches long, but sometimes growing to the weight of 60 pounds. It is called also thromis and corvo.
umbrage: A shade; a screen of trees; as the umbrage of woods.
Shadow; shade; slight appearance. The opinion carries no show of truth nor umbrage of reason on its side.
Suspicion of injury; offense; resentment. The court of France took umbrage at the conduct of spain.
umbrageous: Shading; forming a shade; as umbrageous trees or foliage.
Shady; shaded; as an umbrageous grotto or garden. Umbrageous’ grots, and caves of cool recess.
umbrageousness: Shadiness; as the umbrageousness of a tree.
umbratic: Keeping in the shade or at home.
umbratical: Keeping in the shade or at home.
umbratious: Suspicious; apt to distrust; captious; disposed to take umbrage.
umbrel: A shade, screen or guard, carried in the hand for sheltering the person from the rays of the sun, or from rain or snow. It is formed of silk, cotton or other cloth extended on strips of elastic whalebone, inserted in or fastened to a rod or stick.
umbrella: A shade, screen or guard, carried in the hand for sheltering the person from the rays of the sun, or from rain or snow. It is formed of silk, cotton or other cloth extended on strips of elastic whalebone, inserted in or fastened to a rod or stick.
umpirage: The power, right or authority of an umpire to decide.
umpire: A third person called in to decide a controversy or question submitted to arbitrators, when the arbitrators do not agree in opinion.

A person to whose sole decision a controversy or question between parties is referred. Thus the emperor of Russia was constituted umpire between Great Britain and the United States, to decide the controversy respecting the slaves carried from the states by the British troops.
To arbitrate; to decide as umpire; to settle, as a dispute.
un: a prefix or inseparable preposition, un or on, usually un, an, is the same word as the L. in. It is a particle of negation, giving to words to which it is prefixed, a negative signification. We use un or in indifferently for this purpose; and the tendency of modern usage is to prefer the use of in, in some words, where un was formerly used. Un admits of no change of n into I, m or r, as in does, in illuminate, immenseIt is prefixed generally to adjectives and participles, and almost at pleasure. In a few instances, it is prefixed to verbs, as in unbend, unbindAs the compounds formed with un are so common and so well known, the composition is not noticed under the several words. For the etymologies, see the simple words.
unabashed: Not abashed; not confused with shame, or by modesty.
unabated: Not abated; not diminished in strength or violence. The fever remains unabated.
unabjured: Not abjured; not renounced on oath.
unable: Not able; not having sufficient strength or means; impotent; weak in power, or poor in substance. A man is unable to rise when sick; he is unable to labor; he is unable to support his family or to purchase a farm; he is unable for a particular enterprise.
Not having adequate knowledge or skill. A man is unable to paint a good likeness; he is unable to command a ship or an army.
unabolishable: Not abolishable; that may not be abolished, annulled or destroyed.
unabolished: Not abolished; not repealed or annulled; remaining in force.
unabsolved: s as z. Not absolved; not acquitted or forgiven.
unabsorbable: Not absorbable; not capable of being absorbed.
unaccented: Not accented; having no accent; as an unaccented syllable.
unacceptable: Not acceptable; not pleasing; not welcome; not such as will be received with pleasure.
unacceptably: In an unwelcome or unpleasing manner.
unaccessibleness: State of not being approachable; inaccessibleness.
unaccommodated: Not accommodated; not furnished ~~ with external conveniences.
unaccommodating: Not accommodating; not ready to oblige; uncompliant.
unaccompanied: Not attended; having no attendants, companions or followers.
unaccomplished: Not refined in manners; not furnished with elegant literature or with polish of manners.
unaccountability: The state or quality of not being accountable; or the state of being unaccountable for.
unaccountable: Not to be accounted for. Such folly is unaccountable.
Not explicable; not to be solved by reason or the light possessed; not reducible to rule. The union of soul and body is to us unaccountable.
Not subject to account or control; not subject to answer; not responsible.
unaccountably: In a manner not to be explained; strangely.
unaccredited: Not accredited; not received; not authorized. The minister or the consul remained unaccredited.
unaccused: s as z. Not accused; not charged with a crime or fault.
unaccustomed: Not accustomed; not used; not made familiar; not habituated; as a bullock unaccustomed to the yoke. Jeremiah 31:18.
New; not usual; not made familiar; as unaccustomed air; unaccustomed ideas.
unachievable: That cannot be done or accomplished.
unachieved: Not achieved; not accomplished or performed.
unacknowledged: Not acknowledged; not recognized; as an unacknowledged agent or consul.
Not owned; not confessed; not avowed; as an unacknowledged crime or fault.
unacquaintance: Want of acquaintance or familiarity; want of knowledge; followed by with; as an utter unacquaintance with his design.
unacquainted: Not well known; unusual. And th’ unacquainted light began to fear.
Not having familiar knowledge; followed by with. My ears are unacquainted with such bold truths.
unacted: Not acted; not performed; not executed.
unaddicted: Not addicted; not given or devoted.
unadjusted: Not adjusted; not settled; not regulated; as differences unadjusted.
Not settled; not liquidated; as unadjusted accounts.
unadmired: Not admired; not regarded with great affection or respect.
unadmonished: Not admonished; not cautioned, warned or advised.
unadopted: Not adopted; not received as one’s own.
unadorned: Not adorned; not decorated; not embellished.
unadventurous: Not adventurous; not bold or resolute.
unadvisable: s as z. Not advisable; not to be recommended; not expedient; not prudent.
unadvised: Done without due consideration; rash; as an unadvised measure or proceeding.
unadvisedly: s as z. Imprudently; indiscreetly; rashly; without due consideration.
unaffable: Not affable; not free to converse; reserved.
unaffected: Not affected; plain; natural; not labored or artificial; simple; as unaffected ease and grace./ .
Real; not hypocritical; sincere; as unaffected sorrow.
Not moved; not having the heart or passions touched. Men often remain unaffected under all the solemn monitions — of Providence.
unaffectedly: Really; in sincerity; without disguise; without attempting to produce false appearances. He was unaffectedly cheerful.
unaffecting: Not pathetic; not adapted to move the passions.
unaidable: Not to be aided or assisted.
unaiming: Having no particular aim or direction.
unaking: Not aking; not giving or feeling pain.
unalarmed: Not alarmed; not disturbed with fear.
unalienable: Not alienable; that cannot be alienated; that may not be transferred; as unalienable rights.
unalienably: In a manner that admits of no alienation; as property unalienable vested.
unalienated: Not alienated; not transferred; not estranged.
unallayed: Not allayed; not appeased or quieted.
unalliable: That cannot be allied or connected in amity.
unallied: Having no alliance or connection, either by nature, marriage or treaty; as unallied families or nations, or substances.
unalloyed: Not alloyed; not reduced by foreign admixture; as metals unalloyed. I enjoyed unalloyed satisfaction in his company.
unambiguous: Not ambiguous; not of doubtful meaning; plain; clear; certain.
unambitious: Not affecting show; not showy or prominent; as unambitious ornaments.
unamiable: Not amiable; not conciliating love; not adapted to gain affection.
unamused: s as z. Not amused; not entertained.
unamusing: s as z. Not amusing; not affording entertainment.
unanalysed: s as z. Not analysed; not resolved into simple parts.
unanimated: Not animated; not possessed of life.
Not enlivened; not having spirit; dull.
unanimity: Agreement of a number of persons in opinion or determination; as, there was perfect unanimity among the members of the council.
unanimous: Being of one mind; agreeing in opinion or determination; as, the house of assembly was unanimous; the members of the council were unanimous.
Formed by unanimity; as a unanimous’ vote.
unanimousness: The state of being of one mind.
Proceeding from unanimity; as the unanimousness of a vote.
unannealed: Not annealed; not tempered by heat; suddenly cooled.
unanswerable: Not to be satisfactorily answered; not capable of refutation; as an unanswerable argument.
unanswerably: In a manner not to be answered; beyond refutation.
unanswered: Not answered; not opposed by a reply.
unapocryphal: Not apocryphal; not of doubtful authority.
unappalled: Not appalled; not daunted; not impressed with fear. With eyes erect and visage unappall’d.
unappealable: Not appealable; admitting no appeal; that cannot be carried to a high court by appeal; as an appealable cause.
unappeasable: Not to be appeased or pacified; as an unappeasable clamor.
unappeased: s as z. Not appeased; not pacified.
unapplied: Not applied; not used according to the destination; as unapplied funds.
unapposite: s as z. Not apposite; not suitable.
unapprehensive: Not apprehensive; not fearful or suspecting.
Not intelligent; not ready of — conception.
unapprised: s as z. Not apprised; not previously informed.
unapproached: Not approached; not to be approached.
unappropriated: Not appropriated; not applied or directed to be applied to any specific object; as money or funds.
Not granted or given to any person, company or corporation; as unappropriated lands.
unapproved: Not approved; not having received approbation.
unapt: Not apt; not ready or propense. A soldier, unapt to weep.
Unfit; not qualified; not disposed; with to, before a verb, and for, before a noun; as unapt to admit a conference with reason. Unapt for noble, wise, spiritual employments.
unaptness: Unreadiness; disqualification; want of propension. The mind, by excess of exertion, gets an unaptness to vigorous attempts.
unargued: Not disputed; not opposed by argument.
unarm: To disarm; to strip of armor or arms.
unarmed: Not having an arms or armor; not equipped. Man is born unarmed. It is mean to attack even an enemy unarmed.
Not furnished with scales, prickles or other defense; as animals and plants.
unarraigned: Not arraigned; not brought to trial.
unarranged: Not arranged; not disposed in order.
unartful: Not artful; artless; not having cunning.
unartfully: Without art; in an unartful manner.
unartificial: Not artificial; not formed by art.
unartificially: Not with art; in a manner contrary to art.
unascertainable: That cannot be ascertained, or reduced to a certainty; that cannot be certainly known. The trustees are unascertainable.
unascertained: Not reduced to a certainty; not certainly known.
unasked: Not asked; unsolicited; as, to bestow favors unasked; that was an unasked favor.
Not sought by entreaty or care. The bearded corn ensu’d from earth unask’d.
unassailable: Not assailable; that cannot be assaulted.
unassailed: Not assailed; not attacked by violence. To keep my life and honor unassail’d.
unassayed: Not subjected to assay or trial.
unasserted: not asserted; not affirmed; not vindicated.
unassignable: Not assignable; that cannot be transferred by assignment or indorsement.
unassigned: Not assigned; not declared; not transferred.
unassimilated: Not assimilated; not made to resemble.
In physiology, not formed or converted into a like substance; not animalized, as food.
unassisted: Not assisted; not aided or helped; as unassisted reason.
unassociated: Not associated; not united with a society.
In Connecticut, not united with an association; as an unassociated church.
unassorted: Not assorted; not distributed into sorts.
unassuming: Not assuming; not bold or forward; not making lofty pretensions; not arrogant; modest; as an unassuming youth; unassuming manners.
unassured: Not assured; not confident; as an unassured countenance.
Not to be trusted; as an unassured foe.
Not insured against loss; as goods unassured.
unatonable: Not to be appeased; not to be reconciled.
unatoned: Not expiated. A brother’s blood yet unaton’d.
unattached: Not closely adhering; having no fixed interest; as unattached to any partys .
unattainable: Not to be gained or obtained; as unattainable good.
unattainableness: The state of being beyond the reach or power.
unattempted: Not attempted; not tried; not essayed. Things unattempted yet in prose or = rhyme.
unattended: Not attended; not accompanied; having no retinue or attendance.
Not medically attended; not dressed; as unattended wounds.
unattending: Not attending or listening; not being attentive. lll is lost that praise that is address’d to unattending ears.
unattracted: Not attracted; not affected by attraction.
unaugmented: Not augmented or increased; in grammar, having no augment, or additional syllable.
unauthentic: Not authentic; not genuine or true.
unauthenticated: Not authenticated; not made certain by authority.
unauthorized: Not authorized; not warranted by proper authority; not duly commissioned.
unavailable: Not available; not having sufficient power to produce the intended effect; not effectual; vain; useless.
unavailing: Not having the effect desired; ineffectual; useless; vain; as unavailing efforts; unavailing prayers.
unavenged: Not avenged; not having obtained satisfaction; as, a person is unavenged.
Not punished; as, a crime is unavenged.
unavoidable: That cannot be made null or void.
Not avoidable; not to be shunned; inevitable; as unavoidable evils.
Not to be missed in ratiocination.
unavoidableness: That state of being unavoidable; inevitableness.
unavoidably: Inevitably; in a manner that prevents failure or escape.
unavowed: Not avowed; not acknowledged; not owned; not confessed.
unawaked: Not awakened; not roused from sleep.
Not roused from spiritual slumber or stupidity.
unawakened: Not awakened; not roused from sleep.
Not roused from spiritual slumber or stupidity.
unaware: Suddenly; unexpectedly; without previous preparation. The evil came upon us unawares.
Without premeditated design. He killed the man unawares. At unawares. He breaks at unawares upon our walks.
unawares: Suddenly; unexpectedly; without previous preparation. The evil came upon us unawares.
Without premeditated design. He killed the man unawares. At unawares. He breaks at unawares upon our walks.
unawed: Not awed; not restrained by fear; undaunted.
unbacked: Not having been backed; as an unbacked colt.
Not tamed; not taught to bear a rider .
unbalanced: Not balanced; not poised; not in equipoise. Let earth unbalanc’d from her orbit fly.
Not adjusted; not settled; not brought to an equality of debt and credit; as an unbalanced account.
Not restrained by equal power; as unbalanced parties.
unballast: To free from ballast; to discharge the ballast from.
unballasted: Freed from ballast. 2.a. Not furnished with ballast; not kept steady by ballast or by weight; unsteady; as unballasted wits. “Unballast vessel,” for unballasted, in Addison, is an unauthorized phrase.
unbanded: Stripped of a band; having no band.
unbar: To remove a bar or bars from; to unfasten; to open; as, to unbar a gate.
unbattered: Not battered; not bruised or injured by blows.
unbay: To open; to free from the restraint of mounds. I ought to unbay the current of my passions.
unbeaten: Not beaten; not treated with blows.
Untrod; not beaten by the feet; as unbeaten paths.
unbecome: Not to become; not to be suitable to; to misbecome.
unbecoming: Unsuitable; improper for the person or character; indecent; indecorous. My grief lets unbecoming speeches fall.
unbecomingness: Unsuitableness to the person, character or circumstances; impropriety; indecorousness.
unbed: To raise or rouse from bed. Eels unbed themselves and stir at the noise of thunder.
unbefriended: unbefrend’ed. Not befriended; not supported by friends; having no friendly aid.
unbeguile: To undeceive; to free from the influence of deceit.
unbeheld: Not beheld; not seen; not visible.
unbelief: Incredulity; the withholding of belief; as, unbelief is blind.

In the New Testament, disbelief of the truth of the gospel, rejection of Christ as the Savior of men, and of the doctrines he taught; distrust of God’s promises and faithfulnessMatthew 13:58; Mark 16:14, Hebrews 3:12, Romans 4:20.
unbelieve: To discredit; not to believe or trust.
Not to think real or true.
unbeliever: An incredulous person; one who does not believe.
An infidel; one who discredits revelation, or the mission, character and doctrines of Christ.2 Corinthians 6:14.
unbelieving: Infidel; discrediting divine revelation, or the mission, character and doctrines of Christ; as the unbelieving Jews.Acfs 14:2; Revelation 21:8.
unbend: To free from flexure; to make straight; as, to unbend a bow.
To relax; to remit from a strain or from exertion; to set at ease for a time; as, to unbend the mind from study or care.
To relax effeminately. You unbend your noble strength.
In seamanship, to take the sails from their yards and stays; also, to cast loose a cable from the anchors; also, to untie one rope from another.
unbending: Relaxing from any strain; remitting; taking from their yards, as sails.
Unyielding; resolute; inflexible; applied to persons.
Unyielding; inflexible; firm; applied to things; as unbending truth.
Devoted to relaxation. I hope it may entertain your lordship at an unbending hour.
unbeneficed: Not enjoying or having a benefice.
unbenign: Not benign; not favorable or propitious; malignant.
unbent: Relaxed; remitted; relieved from strain or exertion.
In seamen’s language, taken from the yards; loosed; as, the sails are unbent; the cable is unbent.
Not strained; unstrung; as a bow unbent.
Not crushed; not subdued; as, the soul is unbent by woes.
unbequeathed: Not bequeathed; not given by legacy.
unbesought: unbesaut’. Not besought; not sought by petition or entreaty.
unbestarred: Not adorned or distinguished by stars.
unbestowed: Not bestowed; not given; not disposed of.
unbias: To free from bias or prejudice. The truest service a private man can do his country, is to unbias his mind, as much as possible, between the rival powers.W/ .
unbiased: Freed from prejudice or bias. 2.a. Free from any undue partiality or prejudice; impartial; as an unbiased mind; unbiased opinion or decision.
unbid: Spontaneous; as, thorns shall be earth produce unbid.
Uninvited; not requested to attend; as unbidden guests.
unbidden: Spontaneous; as, thorns shall be earth produce unbid.
Uninvited; not requested to attend; as unbidden guests.
unbind: To untie; to remove a band from; to unfasten; to loose; to set free from shackles. Unbind your fillets; unbind the prisoner’s arms; unbind the load.
unbit: In seamanship, to remove the turns of a cable from off the bits.
unblamableness: State of being chargeable with no blame or fault.
unblamably: In such a manner as to incur no blame. 1 Thessalonians 2:10.
unblasted: Not blasted; not made to wither.
unbleeding: Not bleeding; not suffering loss of blood.
unblemished: Not blemished; not stained; free from turpitude or reproach; in a moral sense; as an unblemished reputation or life.
unblenched: Not disgraced; not injured by any stain or soil; as unblenched majesty.
unblown: Not blown; not having the bud expanded.
unblunted: Not made obtuse or dull; not blunted.
unblushing: Not blushing; destitute of shame; impudent.
unbodied: Having no material body; incorporeal; as unbodied spirits.
unbolt: To remove a bolt from; to unfasten; to open; as, to unbolt a gate.
unbolted: Unsifted; not bolted; not having the bran or coarse part separated by a bolter; as unbolted meal.
unbookish: Not addicted to books or reading.
unborn: Not born; not brought into life; future. Some unborn sorrow, ripe in fortune’s womb. The woes to come, the children yet unborn shall feel this day.
unborrowed: Not borrowed; genuine; original; native; one’s own; as unborrowed beauties; unborrowed gold; unborrowed excellence.
unbosom: To disclose freely one’s secret opinions or feelings.
unbosomed: Disclosed, as secrets; revealed in confidence.
unbosoming: Disclosing, as secrets; revealing in confidence.
unbottomed: Having no bottom; bottomless. The dark, unbottom’d, infinite abyss.
unbought: Not bought; obtained without money or purchase. The unbought dainties of the poor.
unbound: Not bound; loose; wanting a cover; as unbound books.
Not bound by obligation or covenant.
unbounded: Having no bound or limit; unlimited in extent; infinite; interminable; as unbounded space; unbounded power.
Having no check or control; unrestrained. The young man has unbounded license. His extravagance is unbounded.
unbowel: To deprive of the entrails; to exenterate; to eviscerate.
unbrace: To loose; to relax; as, to unbrace a drum; to unbrace the arms; to unbrace the nerves.
unbraid: To separate the strands of a braid; to disentangle.
unbraided: Disentangled, as the strands of a braid.
unbraiding: Separating the strands of a braid.
unbranched: Not ramified; not shooting into branches.
unbreast: unbrest’. To disclose or lay open.
unbred: Not well bred; not polished in manners; ill educated; rude; as unbred minds; unbred servants.
Not taught; as unbred to spinning.
unbribed: Not bribed; not corrupted by money; not unduly influenced by money or gifts.
unbridled: a. Unrestrained; licentious; as unbridled lust; unbridled boldness; unbridled passions.
unbroke: Not broken; not violated. Preserve your vows unbroken.
Not weakened; not crushed; not subdued. How broad his shoulders spread, by age unbroke.
Not tamed; not taught; not accustomed to the saddle, harness or yoke; as an unbroken horse or ox.
unbroken: Not broken; not violated. Preserve your vows unbroken.
Not weakened; not crushed; not subdued. How broad his shoulders spread, by age unbroke.
Not tamed; not taught; not accustomed to the saddle, harness or yoke; as an unbroken horse or ox.
unbrotherly: Not becoming a brother; not suitable to the character and relation of a brother; unkind.
unbruised: s as z. Not bruised; not crushed or hurt.
unbuckle: To loose from buckles; to unfasten; as, to unbuckle a shoe; to unbuckle a girdle; to unbuckle a helm.
unbuild: To demolish what is built; to raze; to destroy.
unbild: To demolish what is built; to raze; to destroy.
unburned: Not burnt; not consumed by fire.
Not injured by fire; not scorched.
unburnt: Not burnt; not consumed by fire.
