Knowledge; exact comprehension. Right understanding consists in the perception of the visible or probably agreement or disagreement of ideas.
Intelligence between two or more persons; agreement of minds; union of sentiments. There is a good understanding between the minister and his people.
understandingly: Intelligibly; with full knowledge or comprehension of a question or subject; as, to vote upon a question understandingly; to act or judge understandingly. The gospel may be neglected, but it cannot be understandingly disbelieved.
understrapper: A petty fellow; an inferior agent.
understratum: Subsoil; the bed or layer of earth on which the old or soil rests.
undertake: To engage in; to enter upon; to take in hand; to begin to perform. When I undertook this work, I had a very inadequate knowledge of the extent of my labors.
To covenant or contract to perform or execute. A man undertakes to erect a house, or to make a mile of canal, when he enters into stipulations for that purpose.
To attempt; as when a man undertakes what he cannot perform.
undertaken: of undertake. The work was undertaken at his own expense.
undertaker: One who undertakes; one who engages in any project or business.
One who stipulates or covenants to perform any work for another.
undertaking: Engaging in; taking in hand; beginning to perform; stipulating to execute.

Any business, work or project which a person engages in, or attempts to perform; an enterprise. The canal, or the making of the canal, from the Hudson to lake Erie, a distance of almost four hundred miles, was the greatest undertaking of the kind in modern times. The attempt to find a navigable passage to the Pacific round North America, is a hazardous undertaking, and probably useless to navigation.
undertenant: The tenant of a tenant; one who holds lands or tenements of a tenant.
undertime: Undern-tide; the time after dinner, or in the evening.
undervaluation: The act of valuing below the real worth; rate not equal to the worth.
undervalue: To value, rate or estimate below the real worth.
To esteem lightly; to treat as of little worth . In comparison of the discharge of my duties, I undervalued all designs of authority.
To despise; to hold in mean estimation. I write not this with the least intention to undervalue the other parts of poetry.
Low rate or price; a price less than the real worth.
undervalued: Estimated at less than the real worth; slighted; despised.
undervaluing: Estimating at less than the real worth; slighting; despising.
underwent: of undergo. He underwent severe trials.
underwood: Small trees that grow among large trees.
underwork: To work or labor upon less than is sufficient or proper.
To work at a less price than others in the like employment; as, one mason may underwork another; a shoemaker cannot underwork a joiner.
underworker: One who underworks; or a subordinate workman.
underworking: Destroying clandestinely; working at a less price than others in the like employment.
underwrite: To write under something else. The change I have made, I have here underwritten .
To subscribe. We whose names are underwritten, agree to pay the sums expressed against your respective names.

To subscribe one’s name for insurance; to set one’s name to a policy of insurance, for the purpose of becoming answerable for loss or damage, for a certain premium per cent. Individuals underwrite policies of insurance, as well as companies. The broker who procures insurance, ought not, by underwriting the policy, to deprive the parties of his unbiased testimony.
underwriter: One who insures; an insurer; so called because he underwrites his name to the conditions of the policy.
underwriting: The act or practice of insuring ships, goods, houses
undescendible: Not descendible; not capable of descending to heirs.
undescried: Not descried; not discovered; not seen.
undeserved: s as z. Not deserved; not merited.
undeservedly: Without desert, either good or evil.
undeserving: Not deserving; not having merit. God continually supplies the wants of his undeserving creatures.
Not meriting; with of; as a man undeserving of happiness, or of punishment.
undeservingly: Without meriting any particular advantage or harm.
undesigned: Not designed; not intended; not proceeding from purpose; as, to do an undesigned injury.
undesignedness: Freedom from design or set purpose.
undesigning: Sincere; upright; artless; having no artful or fraudulent purpose. It is base to practice on undesigning minds.
undesirable: s as z. Not to be desired; not to be wished; not pleasing.
undesired: s as z. Not desired, or not solicited.
undestroyed: Not destroyed; not wasted; not ruined.
undetected: Not detected; not discovered; not laid open.
undeterminable: That cannot be determined or decided.
undeterminate: Not determinate; not settled or certain.
undetermined: Not determined; not settled; not decided.
undeterred: Not deterred; not restrained by fear or obstacles.
undeviating: Not deviating; not departing from the way, or from a rule, principle or purpose; steady; regular; as an undeviating course of virtue.
Not erring; not wandering; not crooked.
undigested: Not digested; not subdued by the stomach; crude.
undiplomatic: Not according to the rules of diplomatic bodies.
undirected: Not directed; not guided; left without direction.
Not addressed; not superscribed; as a letter.
undiscerned: Not discerned; not seen; not observed; not descried; not discovered; as truths undiscerned.
undiscernedly: In such a manner as not to be discovered or seen.
undiscernible: That cannot be discerned, seen or discovered; invisible; as undiscernible objects or distinctions.
undiscernibleness: The state or quality of being undiscernible.
undiscernibly: In a way not to be discovered or seen; invisibly; imperceptibly.
undiscerning: Not discerning; not making just distinctions; wanting judgment or the power of discrimination.
undisciplined: Not disciplined; not duly exercised and taught; not subdued to regularity and order; raw; as undisciplined troops; undisciplined valor.
Not instructed; untaught; as undisciplined minds.
undiscording: Not disagreeing; not jarring in music; harmonious; as undiscording voices.
undiscoverable: That cannot be discovered or found out; as a undiscoverable principles.
undiscoverably: In a manner not to be discovered.
undiscovered: Not discovered; not seen; not descried.
undiscrete: Not discrete; not prudent or wise.
undiscussed: Not discussed; not argued or debated.
undisguised: Not disguised; not covered with a mask, or with a false appearance.
undismayed: Not dismayed; not disheartened by fear; not discouraged; as troops undismayed.
undisordered: s as z. Not disordered; not disturbed.
undispensing: Not allowing to be dispensed with.
undisputed: Not disputed; not contested; not called in question; as an undisputed title; undisputed truth.
undissembled: Not dissembled; open; undisguised; unfeigned; as undissembled friendship or piety.
undissembling: Not dissembling; not exhibiting a false appearance; not false.
undissolvable: That cannot be dissolved or melted.
That may not be loosened or broken; as the undissolvable ties of friendship.
undissolving: Not dissolving; not melting; as the undissolving ice of the Alps.
undistinguishable: That cannot be distinguished by the eye; not to be distinctly seen.
Not to be known or distinguished by the intellect, by any peculiar property.
undistinguishably: Without distinction; so as not to be known from each other, or to be separately seen.
undistinguished: Not distinguished; not so marked as to be distinctly known from each other. Undistinguish’d seeds of good and ill.
Not marked by any particular property.
undistinguishing: Making no difference; not discriminating; as undistinguishing favor. Undistinguishing distribution of good and — evil.
undistracted: Not perplexed by contrariety or confusion of thought, desires or concerns.
undistractedly: Without disturbance from contrariety of thoughts or multiplicity of concerns.
undistractedness: Freedom from disturbance or interruption from contrariety or multiplicity of thoughts and concerns.
undisturbed: Free from interruption; not molested or hindered; as undisturbed with company or noise.
Free from perturbation of mind; calm; tranquil; placid; serene; not agitated. To be undisturbed by danger, by perplexities, by injuries received, is a most desirable object.
Not agitated; not stirred; not moved; as the surface of water undisturbed.
undisturbedness: Calmness; tranquillity; freedom from molestation or agitation.
undiverted: Not amused; not entertained or pleased.
undividable: That cannot be divided; not separable; as an undividable scene.
undivided: Not divided; not separated or disunited; unbroken; whole; as undivided attention or affections.
In botany, not lobed, cleft or branched.
undividedly: So as not to be parted.
undivulged: Not divulged; not revealed or disclosed; secret.
undo: To reverse what has been done; to annul; to bring to naught any transaction. We can undo many kinds of work; but we cannot undo crimes, errors or faults. Tomorrow ere the setting sun, she’d all undo what she had done.
To loose; to open; to take to pieces; to unravel; to unfasten; to untie; as, to undo a knot.
To ruin; to bring to poverty; to impoverish. Many are undone by unavoidable losses; but more undo themselves by vices and dissipation, or by indolence.
To ruin, in a moral sense; to bring to everlasting destruction and misery.
undock: To take out of dock; as, to undock a ship.
undoer: One who undoes or brings destruction; one who reverses what has been done; one who ruins the reputation of another.
undoing: Reversing what has been done; ruining.
The reversal of what has been done.
undone: Ruined; destroyed. When the legislature is corrupted, the people are undone.
a. Not done; not performed; not executed. We are apt to leave undone what we ought to do.
undoubted: undout’ed. Not doubted; not called in question; indubitable; indisputable; as undoubted proof; undoubted truth.
undoubtedly: undout’edly. Without doubt; without question; indubitably. I
undoubtful: undout’ful. Not doubtful; not ambiguous; plain; evident.
undoubting: undout’ing. Not doubting; not hesitating respecting facts; not fluctuating in uncertainty; as an undoubting believer; an undoubting faith.
undrained: Not drained; not freed from water.
undramatic: Not dramatic; not according to the rules of the drama, or not suited to the drama.
undramatical: Not dramatic; not according to the rules of the drama, or not suited to the drama.
undrawn: Not drawn; not pulled by an external force.
Not allured by motives or persuasion.
Not taken from the box; as an undrawn. ticket.
undress: To divest of clothes; to strip.
To divest of ornaments, or the attire of ostentation; to disrobe.
undressed: Not pruned; not trimmed; not put in order; as an undressed vineyard.
undried: Not dried; wet; moist; as undried cloth.
Not dried; green; as undried hay; undried hops.
undrooping: Not drooping; not sinking; not despairing.
undue: Not due; not yet demandable of right; as a debt, note or bond undue.
Not right; not legal; improper; as an undue proceeding.
Not agreeable to a rule or standard, or to duty; not proportioned; excessive; as an undue regard to the externals of religion; an undue attachment to forms; an undue rigor in the execution of law.
undulate: Wavy; waved obtusely up and down, near the margin, as a leaf or corol.
To move back and forth, or up and down, as waves; to cause to vibrate. Breath vocalized, that is, vibrated and undulated - .
To vibrate; to move back and forth; to wave; as undulating air.
undulated: Wavy; waved obtusely up and down, near the margin, as a leaf or corol.
undulation: A waving motion or vibration; as the undulations of a fluid, of water or air; the undulations of sound. The undulations of a fluid are propagated in concentric circles.
In medicine, a particular uneasy sensation of an undulatory motion in the heart.
In music, a rattling or jarring of sounds, as when discordant notes are sounded together. It is called also beat.
In surgery, a certain motion of the matter of an abscess when pressed, which indicates its maturity or fitness for opening.
undull: To remove dullness or obscurity; to clear; to purify.
unduly: Not according to duty or propriety.
Not in proper proportion; excessively. His strength was unduly exerted.
unduteous: Not performing duty to parents and superiors; not obedient; as an unduteous child, apprentice or servant.
undutiful: Not obedient; not performing duty; as an undutiful son or subject.
undutifully: Not according to duty; in a disobedient manner.
undutifulness: Want of respect; violation of duty; disobedience; as the undutifulness of children or subjects.
undying: Not subject to death; immortal; as the undying souls of men.
unearned: unern’ed. Not merited by labor or services. Hoping heaven will bless thy slighted fruits, and give thee bread unearn’d.
unearthed: unerth’ed. Driven from a den, cavern or burrow.
uneasily: With uneasiness or pain. He lives uneasily under the burden.
uneasiness: A moderate degree of pain; restlessness; want of ease; disquiet.
Unquietness of mind; moderate anxiety or perturbation; disquietude.
That which makes uneasy or gives trouble; ruggedness; as the uneasiness of the road.
uneasy: Feeling some degree of pain; restless; disturbed; unquiet. The patient is uneasy.
Giving some pain; as an uneasy garment.
Disturbed in mind; somewhat anxious; unquiet. He is uneasy respecting the success of his project. The soul, uneasy and confin’d from home, rests and expatiates in a life to come.
uneatable: Not eatable; not fit to be eaten.
unedifying: Not edifying; not improving to the mind.
unelastic: Not elastic; not having the property of recovering its original state, when bent or forced out of its form.
unelbowed: Not attended by any at the elbow.
unelected: Not elected; not chosen; not preferred.
uneligible: Not proper to be chosen; ineligible.
unemancipated: Not emancipated or liberated from slavery.
unembarrassed: Not embarrassed; not perplexed in mind; not confused. The speaker appeared unembarrassed.
Free from pecuniary difficulties or incumbrances. He or his property is unembarrassed.
Free from perplexing connection; as, the question comes before the court unembarrassed’ with irrelevant matter.
unembodied: Free from a corporeal body; as unembodied spirits.
Not embodied; not collected into a body; as embodied militia.
unemployed: Not employed; not occupied; not busy; at leisure; not engaged.
Not being in use; as unemployed capital or money.
unemulating: Not emulating; not striving to excel.
unenchanted: Not enchanted; that cannot be enchanted.
unendowed: Not endowed; not furnished; not invested; as a man unendowed with virtues.
Not furnished with funds; as an unendowed college or hospital.
unengaged: Not engaged; not bound by covenant or promise; free from obligation to a particular person; as, a lady is unengaged.
Free from attachment that binds; as, her affections are unengaged.
unengaging: Not adapted to engage or win the attention or affections; not inviting.
unenjoyed: Not enjoyed; not obtained; not possessed.
unentangle: To free from complication or perplexity; to disentangle.
unentangled: Disentangled; . 2 .a. Not entangled; not complicated; not perplexed.
unentertaining: Not entertaining or amusing; giving no delight.
unentertainingness: The quality of being unentertaining or dull.
unenthralled: Not enslaved; not reduced to thralldom.
unenvied: Not envied; exempt from the envy of others.
unequable: Different from itself; different at different times; not uniform; diverse; as unequable motions; unequable months or seasons.
unequal: Not equal; not even; not of the same size, length, breadth, quantity; as men of unequal stature; houses of unequal dimensions.
Not equal in strength, talents, acquirements; inferior.
Not equal in age or station; inferior.
Insufficient; inadequate. His strength is unequal to the task.
Partial; unjust; not furnishing equivalents to the different parties; as an unequal peace; an unequal bargain.
unequaled: Not to be equaled; unparalleled; unrivaled; in a good or bad sense; as unequaled excellence; unequaled ingratitude or baseness.
unequally: Not equally; in different degrees; in disproportion to each other.
Not with like sentiments, temper or religious opinions or habits. Corinthians 6:14.
unequivocal: Not equivocal; not doubtful; clear; evident; as unequivocal evidence.
Not ambiguous; not of doubtful signification; not admitting different interpretations; as unequivocal words or expressions.
unequivocally: Without doubt; without room to doubt; plainly; with full evidence.
unerring: Committing no mistake; incapable of error; as, the unerring wisdom of God.
Incapable of failure; certain. He takes unerring aim.
unespied: Not espied; not discovered; not seen.
unessential: Not essential; not absolutely necessary; not of prime importance.
Void of real being; as unessential night.
Something not constituting essence, or not of absolute necessity. Forms are among the unessentials of religion.
unestablish: To unfix; to deprive of establishment.
unevangelical: Not orthodox; not according to the gospel.
uneven: Not even; not level; as an uneven road or way; uneven ground.
Not equal; not of equal length. Hebrew verse consists of uneven feet.
Not uniform; as an uneven temper.
unevenness: Surface not level; inequality of surface; as the unevenness of ground or of roads.
Turbulence; change; want of uniformity; as the unevenness of king Edward’s reign.
Want of uniformity; as unevenness of temper.
unexacted: Not exacted; not taken by force.
unexaminable: Not to be examined or inquired into.
unexamined: Not examined; not interrogated strictly; as a witness.
Not inquired into; not investigated; as a question.
unexampled: Having no example or similar case; having no precedent; unprecedented; unparalleled; as the unexampled love and sufferings of our Savior.
unexceptionable: a2. Not liable to any exception or objection; unobjectionable; as unexceptionable conduct; unexceptionable testimony.
unexceptionableness: State or quality of being unexceptionable.
unexceptionably: In a manner liable to no objection; as a point unexceptionably proved.
unexcised: s as z. Not charged with the duty of excise.
unexecuted: Not performed; not done; as a task, business or project unexecuted.
Not signed or sealed; not having the proper attestations or forms that give validity; as a contract or deed unexecuted.
unexemplary: Not exemplary; not according to example.
unexemplified: Not exemplified; not illustrated by example.
unexempt: Not exempt; not free by privilege.
unexercised: s as z. Not exercised; not practiced; not disciplined; not experienced.
unexerted: Not called into action; not exerted.
unexhausted: Not exhausted; not drained to the bottom, or to the last article.
Not spent; as unexhausted patience or strength.
unexorcised: s as z. Not exorcised; not cast out by exorcism.
unexpected: Not expected; not looked for; sudden; not provided against.
unexpectedly: At a time or in a manner not expected or looked for; suddenly.
unexpectedness: The quality of being unexpected, or of coming suddenly and by surprise.
unexpectorating: Not expectorating; not discharging from the throat or lungs.
unexpended: Not expended; not laid out. There is an unexpended balance of the appropriation.
unexperienced: Not experienced; not versed; not acquainted by trial or practice.
unexpert: Wanting skill; not ready or dextrous in performance.
unexplored: Not explored; not searched or examined by the eye; unknown.
unexposed: Not laid open to view; concealed.
unexpressed: Not expressed; not mentioned or named; not exhibited.
unexpressive: Not having the power of expressing.
unextended: Occupying no assignable space; having no dimensions; as a spiritual, an unextended substance.
unextinct: Not extinct; not being destroyed; not having perished.
unextinguishable: That cannot be extinguished; unquenchable; as unextinguishable fire.
That cannot be annihilated or repressed; as an unextinguishable thirst for knowledge.
unextinguishably: In a manner or degree that precludes extinction.
unextinguished: Not extinguished; not quenched; not entirely repressed.
unfaded: Not faded; not having lost its strength of color.
unfading: Not liable to lose strength or freshness of coloring.
Not liable to wither; as unfading laurels.
unfadingness: The state or quality of being unfading.
unfailing: Not liable to fail; not capable of being exhausted; as an unfailing spring; unfailing sources of supply.
That does not fail; certain; as an unfailing promise.
unfainting: Not fainting; not sinking; not failing under toil.
unfair: Not honest; not impartial; disingenuous; using trick or artifice; as an unfair dealer.
Not honest; not just; not equal; as unfair practices.
Proceeding from trick or dishonesty; as unfair advantages.
unfairly: Not in a just or equitable manner.
unfairness: Dishonest or disingenuous conduct or practice; use of trick or artifice; applied to persons. He is noted for his unfairness in dealing.
Injustice; want of equitableness; as the unfairness of a proceeding.
unfaithful: Not observant of promises, vows, allegiance or duty; violating trust or confidence; treacherous; perfidious; as an unfaithful subject; an unfaithful husband or wife; an unfaithful servant; an unfaithful bailee or agent.
Not performing the proper duty. My feet through wine unfaithful to their weight - .
Negligent of duty; as an unfaithful workman.
unfaithfully: In violation of promises, vows or duty; treacherously; perfidiously.
Negligently; imperfectly; as work unfaithfully done.
unfaithfulness: Neglect or violation of vows, promises, allegiance or other duty; breach of confidence or trust reposed; perfidiousness; treachery; as the unfaithfulness of a subject to his prince or the state; the unfaithfulness of a husband to his wife, or of a wife to her husband; the unfaithfulness of an agent, servant or officer.
unfamiliar: Not accustomed; not common; not rendered agreeable by frequent use.
unfashionable: Not fashionable; not according to the prevailing mode; as unfashionable dress or language.
Not regulating dress or manners according to the reigning custom; as an unfashionable man.
unfashionableness: Neglect of the prevailing mode; deviation from reigning custom.
unfashionably: Not according to the fashion; as, to be unfashionably dressed.
unfashioned: Not modified by art; amorphous; shapeless; not having a regular form; as a lifeless lump unfashioned.
unfasten: To loose; to unfix; to unbind; to untie.
unfathomable: That cannot be sounded by a line; as an unfathomable lake.
So deep or remote that the limit or extent cannot be found. The designs of Providence are often unfathomable.W/ .
unfathomably: So as not to be capable of being sounded.
unfathomed: Not sounded; not to be sounded.
unfavorable: Not favorable; not propitious; not disposed or adapted to countenance or support. We found the minister’s opinion unfavorable to our project. The committee made a report unfavorable to the petitioner.
Not propitious; not adapted to promote any object; as weather unfavorable for harvest.
unfavorableness: Unpropitiousness; unkindness; want of disposition to countenance or promote.
unfavorably: Unpropitiously; unkindly; so as not to countenance, support or promote; in a manner to discourage.
unfeasible: s as z. That cannot be done; impracticable.
unfeathered: Having no feathers; unfledged; implumous; naked of feathers.
unfethered: Having no feathers; unfledged; implumous; naked of feathers.
unfeatured: Wanting regular features; deformed. Visage rough, deform’d, unfeatur’d.
unfed: Not fed; not supplied with food.
unfeed: Not feed; not retained by a fee.
unfeelingly: In an unfeeling or cruel manner.
unfeigned: Not feigned; not counterfeit; not hypocritical; real; sincere; as unfeigned piety to God; unfeigned love to man.
unfeignedly: Without hypocrisy; really; sincerely. He pardoneth all them that truly repent, and unfeignedly believe his holy gospel.
unfence: unfens’. To strip of fence; to remove a fence from.
unfenced: Deprived of a fence. 2.a. Not fenced; not inclosed; defenseless; as a tract of land unfenced.
unfermented: Not fermented; not having undergone the process of fermentation; as liquor.
unfertile: Not fertile; not rich; not having the qualities necessary to the production of good crops.
unfetter: To loose from fetters; to unchain; to unshackle.
To free from restraint; to set at liberty; as, to unfetter the mind.
unfilial: Unsuitable to a son or child; undutiful; not becoming a child.
unfinished: Not finished; not complete; not brought to an end; imperfect; wanting the last hand or touch; as an unfinished house; an unfinished painting.
unfirm: Not stable; not well fixed. With feet unfirm.
unfit: Unqualified; as a man unfit for an office.
To disable; to make unsuitable; to deprive of the strength, skill or proper qualities for any thing. Sickness unfits a man for labor.
To disqualify; to deprive of the moral or mental qualities necessary for any thing. Sin unfits us for the society of holy beings.
unfitness: Want of suitable powers or qualifications, physical or moral; as the unfitness of a sick man for labor, or of an ignorant man for office; the unfitness of sinners for the enjoyments of heaven.
Want of propriety or adaptation to character or place; as unfitness of behavior or of dress.
unfix: To loosen from any fastening; to detach from any thing that holds; to unsettle; to unhinge; as, to unfix the mind or affections.
To make fluid; to dissolve. Nor can the rising sun unfix their frosts.
unfixed: Unsettled; loosened. 2 .a. Wandering; erratic; inconstant; having no settled habitation.
Having no settled view or object of pursuit.
unflagging: Not flagging; not drooping; maintaining strength or spirit.
unflattering: Not flattering; not gratifying with obsequious behavior; not coloring the truth to please.
Not affording a favorable prospect; as, the weather is unflattering.
unfledged: Not yet furnished with feathers; implumous; as an unfledged bird.
Young; not having attained to full growth.
unfleshed: Not fleshed; not seasoned to blood; raw; as an unfleshed hound; unfleshed valor.
unfold: To open folds; to expand; to spread out.
To open any thing covered or close; to lay open to view or contemplation; to disclose; to reveal; as, to unfold one’s designs; to unfold the principles of a science.
To declare; to tell; to disclose. Unfold the passion of my love.
To display; as, to unfold the works of creation.
To release from a fold or pen; as, to unfold sheep.
unfolded: Opened; expanded; revealed; displayed; released from a fold.
unfolding: Opening; expanding; disclosing; displaying; releasing from a fold.
The act of expanding, displaying or disclosing; disclosure.
unforbid: Not forbid; not prohibited; applied to persons.
Allowed; permitted; legal; applied to things.
unforbidden: Not forbid; not prohibited; applied to persons.
Allowed; permitted; legal; applied to things.
unforced: Not forced; not compelled; not constrained.2z
Not feigned; not heightened; natural as unforced passions; unforced expressions of joy.
Not violent; easy; gradual; as an easy and unforced ascent.
Easy; natural; as an unforced posture.
unforcible: Wanting force or strength; as an unforcible expression.
unfordable: Not fordable; that cannot be forded, or passed by wading; as an unfordable river.
unforgiving: Not forgiving; not disposed to overlook or pardon offenses; implacable.
unforgot: Not forgot; not lost to memory.
unforgotten: Not forgot; not lost to memory.
unform: To destroy; to unmake; to decompose or resolve into parts.
unformed: Not molded into regular shape; as unformed matter.
unforsaken: Not forsaken; not deserted; not entirely neglected.
unfortified: Not fortified; not secured from attack by walls or mounds.
Not guarded; not strengthened against temptations or trials; weak; exposed; defenseless; as an unfortified mind.
Wanting securities or means of defense.<t
unfortunate: Not successful; not prosperous; as an unfortunate adventure; an unfortunate voyage; unfortunate attempts; an unfortunate man; an unfortunate commander; unfortunate business.
ungenerous: Not of a noble mind; not liberal; applied to persons; as an ungenerous man or prince.
Not noble; not liberal; applied to things; as an ungenerous act.
Dishonorable; ignominious. The victor never will impose on Cato ungen’rous terms.
ungenial: Not favorable to nature or to natural growth; as ungenial air; ungenial soils. Sullen seas that was th’ ungenial pole.
ungenteel: Not genteel; used of persons; not consistent with polite manners or good breeding; used of manners.
ungentleness: Want of gentleness; harshness; severity; rudeness.
ungeometrical: Not agreeable to the rules of geometry.
ungifted: Not gifted; not endowed with peculiar faculties.
ungilded: Not gilt; not overlaid with gold.
ungilt: Not gilt; not overlaid with gold.
ungird: To loose from a girdle or band; to unbind. Genesis 24:32.
ungirded: Loosed from a girth or band.
ungirding: Loosing from a girdle or band.
unglazed: Not furnished with glass; as, the windows are unglazed; the house is yet unglazed.
Not covered with vitreous matter; as unglazed potters’ ware.
unglorified: Not glorified; not honored with praise or adoration.
unglorious: Not glorious; bringing no glory or honor.
unglue: to separate any thing that is glued or cemented.
ungodliness: Impiety; wickedness; disregard of God and his commands and neglect of his worship; or any positive act of disobedience or irreverence. The wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness. Romans 1:18.
ungodly: Wicked; impious; neglecting the fear and worship of God, or violating his commands. 7 Peter 4:78.
Sinful; contrary to the divine commands; as ungodly deeds. Jude
Polluted by wickedness; as an ungodly day.
ungored: Not gored; not wounded with a horn.
ungorged: Not gorged; not filled; not sated.
ungovernable: That cannot be governed; that cannot be ruled or restrained.
Licentious; wild; unbridled; as ungovernable passions.
ungovernably: So as not to be governed or restrained.
ungoverned: Not subjected to laws or principles; not restrained or regulated; unbridled; licentious; as ungoverned appetite; ungoverned passions.
ungowned: Not having or not wearing a gown.
ungraceful: Not graceful; not marked with ease and dignity; wanting beauty and elegance; as ungraceful manners. Without politeness, learning is ungraceful.
ungracefulness: Want of gracefulness; want of ease and dignity; want of elegance; awkwardness; as ungracefulness of manners.
ungracious: Unacceptable; not well received; not favored. Any thing of grace towards the Irish rebels was as ungracious at Oxford as at London.
ungraciously: With disfavor. The proposal was received ungraciously.
ungrammatical: Not according to the established and correct rules of grammar.
ungrammatically: In a manner contrary to the rules of grammar.
ungranted: Not granted; not bestowed; not transferred by deed or gift; as ungranted lands.
Not granted; not yielded; not conceded in argument.
ungrateful: Not grateful; not feeling thankful for favors.
Not making returns, or making ill returns for kindness.V\ .
Making no returns for culture; as an ungrateful soil .
Unpleasing; unacceptable. Harsh sounds are ungrateful to the ear.
ungratefulness: Ingratitude; want of due feelings of kindness for favors received; ill return for good.
ungrounded: Having no foundation or support; as ungrounded hopes or confidence.
ungroundedly: Without ground or support; without reason.
ungrudgingly: Without ill will; heartily; cheerfully; as, to bestow charity ungrudgingly.
unguarded: Careless; negligent; not attentive to danger; not cautious; as, to be unguarded in conversation.
Negligently said or done; not done or spoken with caution; as an unguarded expression or action.
unguardedly: Without watchful attention to danger; without caution; carelessly; as, to speak or promise unguardedly.
unguent: Ointment; a soft composition used as a topical remedy, as for sores, burns and the like. An unguent is stiffer than a liniment, but softer than a cerate.
unguentous: Like unguent, or partaking of its qualities.
unguessed: Not obtained by guess or conjecture.
unguicular: In botany, of the length of the human nails, or half an inch.
unguiculate: In botany, clawed; having a narrow base; as the petal in a polypetalous corol.
unguiculated: In botany, clawed; having a narrow base; as the petal in a polypetalous corol.
unguided: Not guided; not led or conducted.
unguilty: ungilt’y. Not guilty; not stained with crime; innocent.
unguinous: Oily; unctuous; consisting of fat or oil, or resembling it.
ungula: In geometry, a section or part of a cylinder, cut off by a plane oblique to the base.
unhabitable: That cannot be inhabited by human beings; uninhabitable.
unhacked: Not hacked; not cut, notched or mangled.
unhackneyed: Not hackneyed; not much used or practiced.
unhallow: To profane; to desecrate. The vanity unhallows the virtue.
unhallowed: Profaned; deprived of its sacred character.
a. Profane; unholy; impure; wicked. In the cause of truth, no unhallowed violence - is either necessary or admissible.
unhand: To loose from the hand; to let go.
unhandled: Not handled; not treated; not touched.
unhandsome: Ungraceful; not beautiful. I cannot admit that there is anything unhandsome or irregular in the globe.
unhandy: Not dextrous; not skillful; not ready in the use of the hands; awkward; as a person unhandy at his work.
Not convenient; as an unhandy posture for writing.
unhang: To divest or strip of hangings, as a room.
To take from the hinges; as, to unhang a gate.
unhanged: Not hung upon a gallows; not punished by hanging.
unhung: Not hung upon a gallows; not punished by hanging.
unhappiness: Infelicity; misery. It is our great unhappiness, when any calamities fall upon us, that we are uneasy and dissatisfied.
unhappy: Unfortunate; unlucky. He has been unhappy in his choice of a partner. Affairs have taken an unhappy turn.
Not happy; in a degree miserable or wretched. She is unhappy in her marriage. Children sometimes render their parents unhappy.
Evil; calamitous; marked by infelicity; as an unhappy day. This unhappy morn.
unharassed: Not harassed; not vexed or troubled.
unharbor: To drive from harbor or shelter.
unharbored: Not sheltered, or affording no shelter.
unhardened: Not hardened; not indurated; as metal.
Not hardened; not made obdurate; as the heart.
unhardy: Not hardy; feeble; not able to endure fatigue.
Not having fortitude; not bold; timorous.
unharmful: Not doing harm; harmless; innoxious. Themselves unharmful, let them live unharm’d.
unharmonious: Not having symmetry or congruity; disproportionate.
unharness: To strip of harness; to loose from harness or gear.
To disarm; to divest of armor.
unhatched: Not hatched; not having left the egg.
Not matured and brought to light; not disclosed.
unhazarded: Not hazarded; not put in danger; not exposed to loss; not adventured.
unhead: unhed’. To take out the head of; as, to unhead a cask.
unheaded: unhed’ed. Having the head taken out.
unheading: unhed’ing. Taking out the head of.
unhealthful: Not healthful; injurious to health; insalubrious; unwholesome; noxious; as an unhealthful climate or air.
Abounding with sickness or disease; sickly; as an unhealthful season.
unhealthfulness: The state of being sickly; as the unhealthfulness of the autumn.
unhealthily: unhelth’ily. In an unwholesome or unsound manner.
unhealthiness: Want of health; habitual weakness or indisposition; applied to persons.
Unsoundness; want of vigor; as the unhealthiness of trees or other plants.
Unfavorableness to health; as the unhealthiness of a climate.
unhealthy: Wanting health; wanting a sound and vigorous state of body; habitually weak or indisposed; as an unhealthy person.
Unsound; wanting vigor of growth; as an unhealthy plant.
Sickly; abounding with disease; as an unhealthy season or city: .
Insalubrious; unwholesome; adapted to generate diseases; as an unhealthy climate or country.
unheard: Not heard; not perceived by the ear.
Not admitted to audience. What pangs I feel, unpitied and unheard!
Not known in fame; not celebrated. Nor was his name unheard.
Unheard of; obscure; not known by fame. Unheard of, new; unprecedented.
unheart: To discourage; to depress; to dishearten.
unhedged: Not hedged; not surrounded by a hedge.
unheeded: Not heeded; disregarded; neglected. The world’s great victor passed unheeded by.2z
unhelped: Unassisted; having no aid or auxiliary; unsupported.
unhesitating: Not hesitating; not remaining in doubt; prompt; ready.
unhindered: Not hindered; not opposed; exerting itself freely.
unhinge: To take from the hinges; as, to unhinge a door.
To displace; to unfix by violence.
To unfix; to loosen; to render unstable or wavering; as, to unhinge the mind; to unhinge opinions.
unhoard: To steal from a hoard; to scatter.
unholiness: Want of holiness; an unsanctified state of the heart.
unholy: Not holy; not renewed and sanctified. 2 Timothy 3:2.
Profane; not hallowed; not consecrated; common. Hebrews 10:29.
unhonored: Not honored; not regarded with veneration; not celebrated.
unhoped: Not hoped for; not so probable as to excite hope. With unhop’d success. Unhoped for, unhoped, as above.
unhopeful: Such as leaves no room to hope.
unhorse: unhors’. To throw from a horse; to cause to dismount.
unhorsed: Throwing from a horse; to cause to dismount.
unhostile: Not belonging to a public enemy.
unimprovable: Not capable of improvement, melioration or advancement to a better condition.
Incapable of being cultivated or tiled.
unimprovableness: The quality of being not improvable.
unimproved: Not improved; not made better or wiser; not advanced in knowledge, manners or excellence.
Not used for a valuable purpose. How many advantages unimproved have we to regret!
Not tilled; not cultivated; as unimproved land or soil; unimproved lots of ground.
unimproving: Not improving; not tending to advance or instruct.
uninchanted: Not enchanted; not affected by magic or enchantment; not haunted.
unindifferent: Not indifferent; not unbiased; partial; leaning to one party.
unindorsed: Not indorsed; not assigned; as an unindorsed note or bill.
unindustrious: Not industrious; not diligent in labor, study or other pursuit.
uninfected: Not infected; not contaminated or affected by foul infectious air.
uninfectious: Not infectious; not foul; not capable of communicating disease.
uninflamed: Not inflamed; not set on fire.
uninflammable: Not inflammable; not capable of being set on fire.
uninfluenced: Not influenced; not persuaded or moved by others, or by foreign considerations; not biased; acting freely.:
Not proceeding from influence, bias or prejudice; as uninfluenced conduct or actions.
uningenuous: Not ingenuous; not frank or candid; disingenuous.
uninhabitable: Not inhabitable; that in which men cannot live; unfit to be the residence of men.
uninhabited: Not inhabited by men; having no inhabitants.
uninjured: Not injured; not hurt; suffering no harm.
uninquisitive: s as z. Not inquisitive; not curious to search and inquire.
uninspired: Not having received any supernatural instruction or illumination.
uninstructed: Not instructed or taught; not educated.
Not directed by superior authority; not furnished with instructions.
uninsulated: Not insulated; not being separated or detached from every thing else.
uninsured: Not insured; not assured against loss.
unintelligent: Not having reason or consciousness; not possessing understanding.
unintelligibility: The quality of being not intelligible.
unintelligible: Not intelligible; that cannot be understood.
unintelligibly: In a manner not to be understood.
unintentional: Not intentional; not designed; done or happening without design.
uninterested: Not interested; not having any interest or property in; having nothing at stake; as, to be uninterested in any business or calamity.
Not having the mind or the passions engaged; as, to be uninterested in a discourse or narration.
uninteresting: Not capable of exiting an interest, or of engaging the mind or passions; as an uninteresting story or poem.
unintermitted: Not intermitted; not interrupted; not suspended for a time; continued.
unintermitting: Not intermitting; not ceasing for a time; continuing.
uninterpolated: Not interpolated; not inserted at a time subsequent to the original writing.
uninterrupted: Not disturbed by intrusion or avocation.
unintrenched: Not intrenched; not defended by intrenchments.
unintricated: Not perplexed; not obscure or intricate.
unintroduced: Not introduced; not properly conducted; obtrusive.
uninured: Not inured; not hardened by use or practice.
uninvested: Not converted into some species of property less fleeting than money; as money uninvested.
uninvestigable: That cannot be investigated or searched out.
uninvited: Not invited; not requested; not solicited.
union: The act of joining two or more things into one, and thus forming a compound body or a mixture; or the junction or coalition of things thus united. Union differs from connection, as it implies the bodies to be in contact, without an intervening body; whereas things may be connected by the intervention of a third body, as by a cord or chain. One kingdom, joy and union without end.
Concord; agreement and conjunction of mind, will, affections or interest. Happy is the family where perfect union subsists between all its members.
The junction or united existence of spirit and matter; as the union of soul and body.
Among painters, a symmetry and agreement between the several parts of a painting.
In architecture, harmony between the colors in the materials of a building.
unison: In music, an accordance or coincidence of sounds, proceeding from an equality in the number of vibrations made in a given time by a sonorous body. If two chords of the same matter have equal length, thickness and tension, they are said to be in unison, and their sounds will be in unison. Sounds of very different qualities and force may be in unison; as the sound of a bell may be in unison with a sound of a flute. Unison then consists in sameness of degree, or similarity in respect to gravity or acuteness, and is applicable to any sound, whether of instruments or of the human organs.
A single unvaried note. In unison, in agreement; in harmony.
Sounding alone. Sounds intermix’d with voice, choral or unison.
unisonance: Accordance of sounds. What constitutes unisonance is the equality of the number of vibrations of two sonorous bodies, in equal times.
unisonant: Being in unison; having the same degree of gravity or acuteness.
unit: One; a word which denotes a single thing or person; the least whole number. Units are the integral parts of any large number.
In mathematics, any known determinate quantity, by the constant repetition of which, any other quantity of the same kind is measured.
unitarian: One who denies the doctrine of the trinity, and ascribes divinity to God the Father only. The Arian and Socinian are both comprehended in the term Unitarian.
Pertaining to Unitarians, or to the doctrine of the unity of the Godhead.
unitarianism: The doctrines of Unitarians, who contend for the unity of the Godhead, in opposition to the Trinitarians, and who of course deny the divinity of Christ.
unite: To put together or join two or more things, which make one compound or mixture. Thus we unite the parts of a building to make one structure. The kingdoms of England, Scotland and Ireland united, form one empire. So we unite spirit and water and other liquors. We unite strands to make a rope. The states of North America united, form one nation.
To join; to connect in a near relation or alliance; as, to unite families by marriage; to unite nations by treaty.
To make to agree or be uniform; as, to unite a kingdom in one form of worship; to unite men in opinions.
To cause to adhere; as, to unite bricks or stones by cement.
To join in interest or fellowship. Genesis 49:6.
united: Joined; made to agree; cemented; mixed; attached by growth. United flowers, are such as have the stamens and pistils in the same flower.
uniter: The person or thing that unites.
uniting: Joining; causing to agree; consolidating; coalescing; growing together.
unity: The state of being one; oneness. Unity may consist of a simple substance or existing being, as the soul; but usually it consists in a close junction of particles or parts, constituting a body detached from other bodies. Unity is a thing undivided itself, but separate from ever other thing.
Concord; conjunction; as a unity of proofs.
Agreement; uniformity; as unity of doctrine; unity of worship in a church.
In christian theology, oneness of sentiment, affection or behavior. How good and how pleasant it is for brethren to dwell together in unity! Psalm 133:1.
In mathematics, the abstract expression for any unit whatsoever. The number 1 is unity, when it is not applied to any particular object; but a unit, when it is so applied.
univalve: Having one valve only, as a shell or pericarp.
A shell having one valve only. The univalves form one of the three divisions into which shells are usually divided.
univalvular: Having one valve only; as a univalvular pericap or shell.
universal: All; extending to or comprehending the whole number, quantity or space; as universal ruin; universal good; universal benevolence. The universal cause acts not by partial, but by general laws.
Total; whole. From harmony, from heav’nly harmony, this universal frame began.
Comprising all the particulars; as universal kinds.

In botany, a universal umbel, is a primary or general umbel; the first or largest set of rays in a compound umbel; opposed to partial. A universal involucre is placed at the foot of a universal umbel. Universal instrument, is one which measures all kinds of distances, lengths; as the pantometer or holometer./ .& Universal dial, is a dial by which the hour may be found by the sun in any part of the world, or under any elevation of the pole.9Y Universal proposition.

In logic, a universal is complex or incomplex. A complex universal, is either a universal proposition, as “every whole is greater than its parts,” or whatever raises a manifold conception in the mind, as the definition of a reasonable animal. An incomplex universal, is what produces one conception only in the mind, and is a simple thing respecting many; as human nature, which relates to every individual in which it is found.
universalism: In theology, the doctrine or belief that all men will be saved or made happy in a future life.
universalist: One who holds the doctrine that all men will be saved.
universality: The state of extending to the whole; as the universality of a proposition; the universality of sin; the universality of the deluge.
universally: With extension to the whole; in a manner to comprehend all; without exception. Air is a fluid universally diffused. God’s laws are universally binding on his creatures.
universe: The collective name of heaven and earth, and all that belongs to them; the whole system of created things.
university: An assemblage of colleges established in any place, with professors for instructing students in the sciences and other branches of learning, and where degrees are conferred. A university is properly a universal school, in which are taught all branches of learning, or the four faculties of theology, medicine, law and the sciences and arts.
univocal: Having one meaning only. A univocal word is opposed to an equivocal, which has two or more significations.
Having unison of sounds; as the octave in music and its replicates.
Certain; regular; pursuing always one tenor.
univocally: In one term; in one sense. How is sin univocally distinguished into venial and mortal, if the venial be not sin?
univoque: In music, univocal concords are the octave and its recurrences, above or below.
univoke: In music, univocal concords are the octave and its recurrences, above or below.
unjointed: Having no joint or articulation; as an unjointed stem.
unjoyous: Not joyous; not gay or cheerful.
unjust: Not just; acting contrary to the standard of right established by the divine law; not equitable; as an unjust man.
Contrary to justice and right; wrongful; as an unjust sentence; an unjust demand; an unjust accusation.
unjustifiable: Not justifiable; that cannot be proved to be right; not to be vindicated or defended; as an unjustifiable motive or action.
unjustifiableness: The quality of not being justifiable.
unjustifiably: In a manner that cannot be justified or vindicated.
unkennel: To drive from his hole; as, to unkennel a fox.
To rouse from secrecy or retreat.
unkenneled: Driven or let loose from confinement, as a fox or dog.
unkept: Not kept; not retained; not preserved.
Not observed; not obeyed; as a command.
unkind: Not kind; not benevolent; not favorable; not obliging.
unkindly: Unnatural; contrary to nature; as an unkindly crime.
Unfavorably; malignant; as an unkindly fog.
Without kindness; without affection; as, to treat one unkindly.
In a manner contrary to nature; unnaturally. All works of nature, abortive, monstrous, or unkindly mix’d.
unkindness: Want of kindness; want of natural affection; want of good will.
unknit: To separate threads that are knit; to open; to loose work that is knit or knotted.
unknot: To free from knots; to untie.
unknowing: Not knowing; ignorant; with of. Unknowing of deceit.
unknown: Not known. The author of the invention is unknown.
unlabored: Not produced by labor; as unlabored harvests.W .
Not cultivated by labor; not tilled.
Spontaneous; voluntary; that offers without effort; natural. And from the theme unlabor’d beauties rise.
Easy; natural; not stiff, as an unlabored style.
unlaborious: Not laborious; not difficult to be done.
unlace: To loose from lacing or fastening by a cord or strings passed through loops and holes; as, to unlace a helmet or a garment.
In sea language, to loose and take off a bonnet from a sail.
unlade: To unload; to take out the cargo of; as, to unlade a ship.
To unload; to remove, as a load or burden. Acts 27:3.
unlaid: Not allayed; not pacified; not suppressed.
Not laid out, as a corpse.
unlamented: Not lamented; whose loss is not deplored. Thus unlamented pass the proud away.
unlarded: Not intermixed or inserted for improvement.
unlatch: To open or loose by lifting the latch.
unlaureled: Not crowned with laurel; not honored.
unlavish: Not lavish; not profuse; not wasteful.
unlaw: To deprive of the authority of law.
unlawful: Not lawful; contrary to law; illegal; not permitted by law. Unlawful assembly, in law, the meeting of three or more persons to commit an unlawful act.
unlawfully: In violation of law or right; illegally; .
Illegitimately; not in wedlock; as a child unlawfully born.
unlearn: unlern’. To forget or lose what has been learned. It is most important to us all to unlearn the errors of our early education. I had learned nothing right; I had to unlearn everything.
unlearned: Forgotten. 2 .a. Not learned; ignorant; illiterate; not instructed.
Not gained by study; not known.
Not suitable to a learned man; as unlearned verses.
unleavened: unlev’ened. Not leavened; not raised by leaven, barm or yeast. Exodus 12:20.
unless: Except; that is, remove or dismiss the fact or thing stated in the sentence or clause which follows. “We cannot thrive unless we are industrious and frugal.” the sense will be more obvious with the clauses of the sentence inverted. Unless, we are industrious and frugal, we cannot thrive. Unless then answers for a negation. If we are not industrious, we cannot thrive.
unlicensed: Not licensed; not having permission by authority; as an unlicensed innkeeper. The vending of ardent spirits, in places licensed or unlicensed, is a tremendous evil.
unlicked: shapeless; not formed to smoothness; as an unlicked bear whelp.
unlighted: Not kindled or set on fire.
unlike: Dissimilar; having no resemblance. Never were two men more unlike. The cases are entirely unlike.
unlikely: Improbably; such as cannot be reasonably expected; as an unlikely event. The thing you mention is very unlikely.
Not promising success. He employs very unlikely means to effect his object.
unlimber: Not limber; not flexible; not yielding.
unlimited: Not limited; having no bounds; boundless.
Undefined; indefinite; not bounded by proper exceptions; as unlimited terms.
Unconfined; not restrained. Ascribe not to God such as unlimited exercise of mercy as may destroy his justice. Unlimited problem, is one which is capable of infinite solutions.
unlimitedness: The state of being boundless, or of being undefined.
unlineal: Not in a line; not coming in the order of succession.
unlink: To separate links; to loose; to unfasten: to untwist.
unliquidated: Not liquidated; not settled; not having the exact amount ascertained; as an unliquidated debt; unliquidated accounts.
unliquored: Not moistened; not smeared with liquor; not filled with liquor.
unlistening: Not listening; not hearing; not regarding.
unload: To take the load from; to discharge of a load or cargo; as, to unload a ship; to unload a cart.
To disburden; as, to unload a beast.
To disburden; to relieve from any thing onerous or troublesome.
unloaded: Freed from a load or cargo; disburdened.
unloading: Freeing from a load or cargo; disburdening; relieving of a burden.
unlocated: Not place; not fixed in a place.
In America, unlocated lands are such new or wild lands as have not been surveyed, appropriated or designated by marks, limits or boundaries, to some individual, company or corporation.
unlock: To unfasten what is locked; as, to unlock a door or a chest.
To open, in general; to lay open. Unlock your springs, and open all your shades.
unlocked: a. Not locked; not made fast. Unlocked for, not expected; not foreseen.
unloose: unloos’. To fall in pieces; to lose all connection or union.
unlosable: s as z. That cannot be lost.
unloveliness: Want of loveliness; unamiableness; want of the qualities which attract love.
unlovely: Not lovely; not amiable; destitute of the qualities which attract love, or possessing qualities that excite dislike.
unlucky: Unfortunate; not successful; as an unlucky man.
Unfortunate; not resulting in success; as an unlucky adventure; an unlucky throw of dice; an unlucky game.
Unhappy; miserable; subject to frequent misfortunes.
Slightly mischievous; mischievously waggish; as an unlucky boy; an unlucky wag.
Ill omened; inauspicious. Haunt me not with that unlucky face.
unlute: To separate things cemented or luted; to take the lute or clay from.
unmade: Deprived of its form or qualities.
a. Not made; not yet formed.
unmaimed: Not maimed; not disabled in any limb; sound; entire.
unmake: To destroy the form and qualities which constitute a thing what it is. God does not make or unmake things to try experiments.
To deprive of qualities before possessed.
unmaking: Destroying the peculiar properties of a thing.
unmalleability: The quality or state of being unmalleable.
unmalleable: Not malleable; not capable of being hammered into a plate, or of being extended by beating.
unman: To deprive of the constitutional qualities of a human being, as reason.
To deprive of men; as, to unman a ship.
To emasculate; to deprive of virility!
To deprive of the courage and fortitude of a man; to break or reduce into irresolution; to dishearten; to deject.
unmanageable: Not manageable; not easily restrained, governed or directed; not controllable.
unmanlike: Unsuitable to a man; effeminate. Unmanly warmth and tenderness of love.
Not worthy of a noble mind; ignoble; base; ungenerous; cowardly.
unmanly: Unsuitable to a man; effeminate. Unmanly warmth and tenderness of love.
Not worthy of a noble mind; ignoble; base; ungenerous; cowardly.
unmanned: Deprived of the qualities of a man.
unmannerliness: Want of good manners; breach of civility; rudeness of behavior.
unmannerly: Ill bred; not having good manners; rude in behavior; as an unmannerly youth.
Not according to good manners; as an unmannerly jest.
unmanufactured: Not manufactured; not wrought into the proper form for use.
unmanured: Not manured; not enriched by manure.
unmarred: Not marred; not injured; not spoiled; not obstructed.
unmarried: Not married; having no husband or no wife.
unmarshaled: Not disposed or arranged in due order.
unmasculine: Not masculine or manly; feeble; effeminate.
unmask: To strip of a mask or of any disguise; to lay open what is concealed.
unmasked: Stripped of a mask or disguise.
unmasterable: That cannot be mastered or subdued.
unmastered: Not conquerable. He cannot his unmaster’d grief sustain.
unmatchable: That cannot be matched; that cannot be equaled; unparalleled.
unmatched: Matchless; having no match or equal.
unmeaning: Having no meaning or signification; as unmeaning words.
Not expressive; not indicating intelligence; as an unmeaning face. There pride sits blazon’d on the’ unmeaning brow.
unmeasurable: unmezh’urable. That cannot be measured; unbounded; boundless.
unmechanical: Not mechanical; not according to the laws or principles of mechanics. Unmeddled with, not meddled with; not touched; not altered.
unmeddling: Not meddling; not interfering with the concerns of others; not officious.
unmeditated: Not meditated; not prepared by previous thought.
unmeet: Not fit; not proper; not worthy or suitable.
unmeetly: Not fitly; not properly; not suitably.
unmercantile: Not according to the customs and rules of commerce.
unmerchantable: Not merchantable; not of a quality fit for the market.
unmerciful: Not merciful; cruel; inhuman to such beings as are in one’s power; not disposed to spare or forgive.
unmercifulness: Want of mercy; want of tenderness and compassion towards those who are in one’s power; cruelty in the exercise of power or punishment.
unmerited: Not merited; not deserved; obtained without service or equivalent; as unmerited promotion.
Not deserved; cruel; unjust; as unmerited sufferings or injuries.
unmetallic: Not metallic; not having the properties of metal; not belonging to metals.
unmilitary: Not according to military rules or customs.
unmilled: Not milled; not indented or grained; as unmilled coin.
unmindful: Not mindful; not heedful; not attentive; regardless; as unmindful of laws; unmindful of health or of duty.
unmingled: Pure; not vitiated or alloyed by foreign admixture; as unmingled joy.
unmiry: Not miry; not muddy; not foul with dirt.
unmissed: Not missed; not perceived to be gone or lost.
unmitigated: Not mitigated; not lessened; not softened in severity or harshness.
unmixed: Not mixed; not mingled; pure; unadulterated; unvitiated by foreign admixture.
unmixt: Not mixed; not mingled; pure; unadulterated; unvitiated by foreign admixture.
unmodifiable: That cannot be modified or altered in form; that cannot be reduced to a more acceptable or desired form.
unmodified: Not modified; not altered in form; not qualified in meaning.
unmodish: Not modish; not according to custom.
unmold: To change the form; to reduce from any form.
unmolded: a. Not molded; not shaped or formed.
unmolested: Not molested; not disturbed; free from disturbance.
unmoor: In sea language, to bring to the state of riding with a single anchor, after having been moored by two or more cables.
unmoored: Loosed from anchorage, or brought to ride with a single anchor.
unmooring: Loosing from anchorage, or bringing to ride with a single anchor.
unmoralized: Untutored by morality; not conformed to good morals.
unmortified: Not subdued by sorrow; as unmortified sin.
unmounted: Not mounted. Unmounted dragoons are such as have not horses.
unmovable: That cannot be moved or shaken; firm; fixed.
unmoved: Not moved; not transferred from one place to another.
Not changed in purpose; unshaken; firm. \WWV”AD .
Not affected; not having the passions excited; not touched or impressed.

unmoving: Not exciting emotion; having no power to affect the passions.
